title: Which countries have banned chick culling?
subtitle: Chick culling is the process of separating and killing unwanted male and unhealthy female chicks that cannot produce eggs in industrialized egg facilities.
note: In Switzerland grinding is banned but gassing is still allowed. Belgium has only a regional ban.
caption:
Which countries have banned chick culling? 2026

Chick culling is the process of separating and killing unwanted male and unhealthy female chicks that cannot produce eggs in industrialized egg facilities.

This choropleth world map displays the regulatory status of chick culling across various countries as of 2026. The legend categorizes nations into five distinct groups: "Banned" (dark blue), "Banned but not yet in effect" (green), "Not banned" (brown), "Partially banned" (purple), and "No data" (hatched diagonal lines). The vast majority of the world, including North America, South America, Africa, Asia, and Australia, is colored brown, indicating that chick culling is not banned in these regions. A large portion of Europe also falls into this "Not banned" category. In contrast, a cluster of countries in Western and Central Europe is shaded dark blue, signifying that the practice is banned; this group includes France, Germany, and the Netherlands. Italy appears in green, showing a ban that is not yet in effect. Switzerland is colored purple, representing a partial ban where grinding is prohibited but gassing remains allowed. Belgium is also shown in the partial ban category, reflecting a regional restriction rather than a national one. The remaining countries across the globe are covered in diagonal hatching, denoting that no data is available regarding their specific regulations on this practice. The visual distribution highlights that while bans exist, they are currently concentrated within a specific region of Europe, while the rest of the mapped world largely permits the practice or lacks reported data.
